title: Bridge Essay: Visual mental imagery: Evidence for a heterarchical neural architecture.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through Visual, mental, imagery:, Evidence, for.

**Visual mental imagery: Evidence for aheterarchical neural architecture.** — Phys Life Rev. PMID: 38217888. DOI: 10.1016/j.plrev.2023.12.012. Theories of Visual Mental Imagery (VMI) emphasize the processes of retrieval, modification, and recombination of sensory information from long-term memory. Yet, only few studies have focused on the behavioral mechanisms and neural correlates supporting VMI of stimuli from different semantic domains. Therefore, we currently have alimited understanding of how the brain generates and maintains mental representations of colors, faces, shapes - to name afew. Such an undetermined scenario renders un
